title: Odontoglossum zelenkoanum
taxonomy: Odontoglossum zelenkoanum is a species of orchid in the subtribe Oncidiinae. This subtribe is part of the tribe Cymbidieae, which is within the family Orchidaceae. The Oncidiinae subtribe is characterized by its epiphytic habit and a wide range of flower colors. Within the Oncidiinae, Odontoglossum zelenkoanum is classified as a member of the genus Odontoglossum, which is now often merged with the genus Miltonia and other genera. The classification of Odontoglossum zelenkoanum is subject to ongoing research and revisions in the field of orchid taxonomy.

intro: Odontoglossum zelenkoanum is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of South America, specifically found in the Andean highlands of Peru and Bolivia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with health benefits including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, as well as its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Odontoglossum zelenkoanum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Odontoglossum zelenkoanum is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used in traditional remedies for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects.
